title: What is the pathway to citizenship: level 1
description: Test your knowledge on the pathway to citizenship!
topic_path: What is the pathway to citizenship?
What is the pathway to citizenship: level 1

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements defines naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the legal process that a person goes through to become a citizen
the process by which unique groups of people make collective decisions
the principles that determine how people obtain citizenship at birth
the promise that immigrants make to be loyal to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines a Green Card?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the identification given to lawful permanent residents that authorizes them to live in the United States indefinitely
the documentation given to undocumented immigrants brought as children to prevent their deportation
the credentials a refugee needs to bring to the United States before they can enter the United States
the document that the government issues that allows a person to travel to and from countries

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines a naturalized citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person who was not a citizen of a country at birth but became one later
a person allegiant to two or more countries at the same time
a person who inherited their citizenship status from their parents
a person who has the protection of a nation but is not a recognized member

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines a lawful permanent resident?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person who is not a citizen of a country but has authorization to live there indefinitely
a person who has moved to another country without getting the proper authorization from that country’s immigration authorities
a person under the protection of a nation without having the formal status of a citizen
a person who meets the definition of a refugee and is seeking admission into a country at a port of entry

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines a visa?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a document that gives a person legal permission to enter, leave, or stay in a country for a time frame
a temporary order that prevents a person from deportation from the United States
an identification card that allows immigrants to stay in the United States indefinitely
a sworn declaration that a person says that promises they will be loyal to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which branch of government has power to determine the requirements for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Legislative branch
the Executive branch
the Judicial branch
the individual states

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a way in which a person can get citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: live in the United States for five years and pass a citizenship test
give birth to or parent a child that gets birthright citizenship
serve in an international organization like the United Nations
get special amnesty from the president of the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
> 1. Pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States

> 2. Live in the United States for at least five years if they are not married to a citizen

> 3. File a Declaration of Intention with the US Citizenship and Immigration Services

> 4. Complete the citizenship exam on United States civics

> 5. File an application for naturalization and get fingerprinted added to their records

> 6. Interview with an US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) official

**Which sequence of events accurately represents the pathway an immigrant would take toward getting citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

> **STEP 1:** **?**

>**STEP 2:** Live in the United States for at least five years if they are not married to a citizen

>**STEP 3:** File an application for naturalization and get fingerprinted added to their records

>**STEP 4:** Interview with an US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) official

>**STEP 5:** Complete the citizenship exam on United States civics

>**STEP 6:** Pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States

**Which action fills in the missing event in the pathway of citizenship shown above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: determine if you are eligible to become a US citizen
give up citizenship in any other country
complete a literacy test to prove ability to speak English
register as a lawful permanent resident in their state

assessment_items.question_content:
> **STEP 1:** Determine if you are eligible to apply for naturalization. 

>**STEP 2:** Live in the United States for at least five years if they are not married to a citizen

>**STEP 3:** File an application for naturalization and get fingerprinted added to their records

>**STEP 4:** Interview with an US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) official

>**STEP 5:** **?**

>**STEP 6:** Pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States

**Which action fills in the missing event in the pathway of citizenship shown above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: complete a citizenship exam on United States civics
return to original country for approval to transfer citizenship
learn English well enough to read, speak, write, and understand
prove that they are of “good moral character”

assessment_items.question_content:
> **STEP 1:** Determine if you are eligible to apply for naturalization. 

>**STEP 2:** Live in the United States for at least five years if they are not married to a citizen

>**STEP 3:** File an application for naturalization and get fingerprinted added to their records

>**STEP 4:** Interview with an US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) official

>**STEP 5:** Complete the citizenship exam on United States civics 

>**STEP 6:** **?**

**Which action fills in the missing event in the pathway of citizenship shown above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States
file for social security with the federal government
apply for a passport with the United States
register to vote with their state of residence

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following executive branch agencies is responsible for overseeing lawful immigration to the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS)
Immigration and Custom Enforcement (ICE)
Department of Health and Human Services
Department of State

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true about naturalization in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Only the federal government can make immigration laws in the United States.
States have final authority to approve or deny who becomes a citizen of the United States.
Applicants seeking naturalization must return to their home country before they can become naturalized citizens.
Refugees and asylum seekers have no pathway to citizenship in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true about naturalization in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Naturalized citizens have to give up their loyalty to their home country when they become citizens of the United States.
Applicants for naturalization have to answer one hundred questions about United States civics to become a citizen.
Immigrant children under the age of eighteen have to wait until they turn eighteen before they can become citizens.
Any immigrant, regardless of whether they have legal authorization to live in the United States, can be naturalized.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are criteria that an immigrant must meet to become a citizen through naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: be able to read, write, speak, and understand conversational English
have continuously lived in the United States for at least five years
give up any loyalties to any other country they lived in before
be a registered member of one of the two major political parties

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are criteria that a foreign citizen must meet to become a citizen through naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: show that they are of “good moral character”
be at least eighteen years of age
have been lawfully admitted as a permanent resident
register with the United States military for service

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups of people are eligible to get a Green Card?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: people with extraordinary skills like athletes
people with specialized jobs like doctors
people who are married to US citizens
people serving in foreign militaries

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups of people are ineligible for the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: undocumented immigrants
foreign nationals
lawful permanent residents
refugees

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are barriers that people face when going through the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They face wait times to get lawful permanent residence.
They have to wait years to get through the naturalization process.
They deal with quotas that favor immigrants from certain countries.
They must have a high school education or equivalent before applying.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are ways that a person can become a US citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by being born on United States soil
having a parent who is a US citizen
by becoming a naturalized citizen
getting a job in the federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are benefits of becoming a citizen of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: one can run for public office
one is safe from deportation
one does not have to pay taxes
one can opt out of the selective service
